verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now when He was in Jerusalem at the Passover, in the feast, many believed on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of the men of that time some clung to their error, others laid hold on the truth, while of these last, some having retained it for a little while again fell off from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Alluding to these, Christ compared them to seeds not deeply sown, but having their roots upon the surface of the earth; and He said that they should quickly perish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And these the Evangelist has here pointed out to us, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When He was in Jerusalem, at the Passover, in the feast, many believed on Him, when they saw the miracles which He did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But Jesus did not commit Himself unto them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they were the more perfect among His disciples, who came to Him not only because of His miracles, but through His teaching also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The grosser sort the miracles attracted, but the better reasoners His prophecies and doctrines; and so they who were taken by His teaching were more steadfast than those attracted by His miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Christ also called them “blessed,” saying, “Blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.” ( c. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But that these here mentioned were not real disciples, the following passage shows, for it saith, “Jesus did not commit Himself unto them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because He knew all things,”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And needed not that any should testify of man, for He knew what was in man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The meaning is of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He who dwells in men’s hearts, and enters into their thoughts, took no heed of outward words; and knowing well that their warmth was but for a season, He placed not confidence in them as in perfect disciples, nor committed all His doctrines to them as though they had already become firm believers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now, to know what is in the heart of men belongs to God alone, “who hath fashioned hearts one by one” ( Ps. xxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 , LXX.), for, saith Solomon, “Thou, even Thou only, knowest the hearts” ( 1 Kings viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 39 ); He therefore needed not witnesses to learn the thoughts of His own creatures, and so He felt no confidence in them because of their mere, temporary belief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Men, who know neither the present nor the future, often tell and entrust all without any reserve to persons who approach them deceitfully and who shortly will fall off from them; but Christ did not so, for well He knew all their secret thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And many such now there are, who have indeed the name of faith, but are unstable, and easily led away; wherefore neither now doth Christ commit Himself to them, but concealeth from them many things; and just as we do not place confidence in mere acquaintances but in real friends, so also doth Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hear what He saith to His disciples, “Henceforth I call you not servants, ye are My friends.” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence is this and why?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because all things that I have heard of My Father I have made known unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore He gave no signs to the Jews who asked for them, because they asked tempting Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed the asking for signs is a practice of tempters both then and now; for even now there are some that seek them and say, “Why do not miracles take place also at this present time?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou art faithful, as thou oughtest to be, and lovest Christ as thou oughtest to love Him, thou hast no need of signs, they are given to the unbelievers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” asks one, “were they not given to the Jews?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Given they certainly were; and if there were times when though they asked they did not receive them, it was because they asked them not that they might be delivered from their unbelief, but in order the more to confirm their wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And there was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The same came to Jesus by night.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This man appears also in the middle of the Gospel, making defense for Christ; for he saith, “Our law judgeth no man before it hear him” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 51 ); and the Jews in anger replied to him, “Search and look, for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again after the crucifixion he bestowed great care upon the burial of the Lord’s body: “There came also,” saith the Evangelist, “Nicodemus, which came to the Lord by night, and brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.” ( c. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And even now he was disposed towards Christ, but not as he ought, nor with proper sentiments respecting Him, for he was as yet entangled in Jewish infirmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore he came by night, because he feared to do so by day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not for this did the merciful God reject or rebuke him, or deprive him of His instruction, but even with much kindness conversed with him and disclosed to him very exalted doctrines, enigmatically indeed, but nevertheless He disclosed them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For far more deserving of pardon was he than those who acted thus through wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They are entirely without excuse; but he, though he was liable to condemnation, yet was not so to an equal degree.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then does the Evangelist say nothing of the kind concerning him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He has said in another place, that “of the rulers also many believed on Him, but because of the Jews they did not confess (Him), lest they should be put out of the synagogue” ( c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 42 ); but here he has implied the whole by mentioning his coming “by night.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Nicodemus?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Rabbi, we know that Thou art a Teacher come from God: for no man can do the miracles that Thou doest, except God be with him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nicodemus yet lingers below, has yet human thoughts concerning Him, and speaks of Him as of a Prophet, imagining nothing great from His miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We know,” he says, “that Thou art a Teacher come from God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then comest thou by night and secretly, to Him that speaketh the things of God, to Him who cometh from God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why conversest thou not with Him openly?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Jesus said nothing like this to him, nor did He rebuke him; for, saith the Prophet, “A bruised reed shall He not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench; He shall not strive nor cry” ( Isa. xlii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2, 3; as quoted Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19, 20 ): and again He saith Himself, “I came not to condemn the world, but to save the world.” ( c. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “No man can do these miracles, except God be with him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still here Nicodemus speaks like the heretics, in saying, that He hath a power working within Him, and hath need of the aid of others to do as He did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe His exceeding condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He refrained for a while from saying, “I need not the help of others, but do all things with power, for I am the Very Son of God, and have the same power as My Father,” because this would have been too hard for His hearer; for I say now what I am always saying, that what Christ desired was, not so much for a while to reveal His own Dignity, as to persuade men that He did nothing contrary to His Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore in many places he appears in words confined by limits, but in His actions He doth not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He worketh a miracle, He doth all with power, saying, “I will, be thou clean.” ( Matt. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Talitha, arise.” ( Mark v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 41 ; not verbally quoted.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Stretch forth thy hand.” ( Mark iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thy sins be forgiven thee.” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Peace, be still.” ( Mark iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou foul spirit, I say unto thee, come out of him.” ( Mark ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 ; not verbally quoted.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Be it unto thee even as thou wilt.” ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any one say (aught) unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of him.” ( Mark xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This day shalt thou be with Me in Paradise.” ( Luke xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not kill; but I say unto you, that whosoever is angry with his brother without a cause, shall be in danger of the judgment.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Come ye after Me, and I will make you fishers of men.” ( Mark i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And everywhere we observe that His authority is great; for in His actions no one could find fault with what was done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How was it possible?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had His words not come to pass, nor been accomplished as He commanded, any one might have said that they were the commands of a madman; but since they did come to pass, the reality of their accomplishment stopped men’s mouths even against their will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But with regard to His discourses, they might often in their insolence charge Him with madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore now in the case of Nicodemus, He utters nothing openly, but by dark sayings leads him up from his low thoughts, teaching him, that He has sufficient power in Himself to show forth miracles; for that His Father begat Him Perfect and All-sufficient, and without any imperfection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us see how He effects this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nicodemus saith, “Rabbi, we know that Thou art a Teacher come from God, for no man can do the miracles that Thou doest, except God be with him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He thought he had said something great when he had spoken thus of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that he had not yet set foot even on the threshold of right knowledge, nor stood in the porch, but was yet wandering somewhere without the palace, both he and whoever else should say the like, and that he had not so much as glanced towards true knowledge when he held such an opinion of the Only-Begotten, what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the Kingdom of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “Unless thou art born again and receivest the right doctrines, thou art wandering somewhere without, and art far from the Kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He does not speak so plainly as this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order to make the saying less hard to bear, He does not plainly direct it at him, but speaks indefinitely, “Except a man be born again”: all but saying, “both thou and any other, who may have such opinions concerning Me, art somewhere without the Kingdom.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had He not spoken from a desire to establish this, His answer would have been suitable to what had been said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the Jews, if these words had been addressed to them, would have derided Him and departed; but Nicodemus shows here also his desire of instruction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is why in many places Christ speaks obscurely, because He wishes to rouse His hearers to ask questions, and to render them more attentive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that which is said plainly often escapes the hearer, but what is obscure renders him more active and zealous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now what He saith, is something like this: “If thou art not born again, if thou partakest not of the Spirit which is by the washing of Regeneration, thou canst not have a right opinion of Me, for the opinion which thou hast is not spiritual, but carnal.” ( Tit. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He did not speak thus, as refusing to confound one who had brought such as he had, and who had spoken to the best of his ability; and He leads him unsuspectedly up to greater knowledge, saying, “Except a man be born again.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The word “again,” in this place, some understand to mean “from heaven,” others, “from the beginning.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It is impossible,” saith Christ, “for one not so born to see the Kingdom of God”; in this pointing to Himself, and declaring that there is another beside the natural sight, and that we have need of other eyes to behold Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having heard this,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Nicodemus saith, How can a man be born when he is old?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Callest thou Him “Master,” sayest thou that He is “come from God,” and yet receivest thou not His words, but usest to thy Teacher a manner of speaking which expresses much perplexity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the “How,” is the doubting question of those who have no strong belief, but who are yet of the earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore Sarah laughed when she had said, “How?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And many others having asked this question, have fallen from the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And thus heretics continue in their heresy, because they frequently make this enquiry, saying, some of them, “How was He begotten?” others, “How was He made flesh?” and subjecting that Infinite Essence to the weakness of their own reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing which, we ought to avoid this unseasonable curiosity, for they who search into these matters shall, without learning the “How,” fall away from the right faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account Nicodemus, being in doubt, enquires the manner in which this can be, (for he understood that the words spoken referred to himself,) is confused, and dizzy, and in perplexity, having come as to a man, and hearing more than man’s words, and such as no one ever yet had heard; and for a while he rouses himself at the sublimity of the sayings, but yet is in darkness, and unstable, borne about in every direction, and continually falling away from the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore he perseveres in proving the impossibility, so as to provoke Him to clearer teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Can a man,” he saith, “enter into his mother’s womb, and be born?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how when one commits spiritual things to his own reasonings, he speaks ridiculously, seems to be trifling, or to be drunken, when he pries into what has been said beyond what seems good to God, and admits not the submission of faith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nicodemus heard of the spiritual Birth, yet perceived it not as spiritual, but dragged down the words to the lowness of the flesh, and made a doctrine so great and high depend upon physical consequence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so he invents frivolities, and ridiculous difficulties.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul said, “The natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit.” ( 1 Cor. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet even in this he preserved his reverence for Christ, for he did not mock at what had been said, but, deeming it impossible, held his peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There were two difficulties; a Birth of this kind, and the Kingdom; for neither had the name of the Kingdom ever been heard among the Jews, nor of a Birth like this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he stops for a while at the first, which most astonished his mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, knowing this, not enquire into things relating to God by reasoning, nor bring heavenly matters under the rule of earthly consequences, nor subject them to the necessity of nature; but let us think of all reverently, believing as the Scriptures have said; for the busy and curious person gains nothing, and besides not finding what he seeks, shall suffer extreme punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, that (the Father) begat (the Son): believe what thou hast heard; but do ask not, “How,” and so take away the Generation; to do so would be extreme folly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if this man, because, on hearing of a Generation, not that ineffable Generation, but this which is by grace, he conceived nothing great concerning it, but human and earthly thoughts, was therefore darkened and in doubt, what punishment must they deserve, who are busy and curious about that most awful Generation, which transcends all reason and intellect?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For nothing causes such dizziness as human reasoning, all whose words are of earth, and which cannot endure to be enlightened from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Earthly reasonings are full of mud, and therefore need we streams from heaven, that when the mud has settled, the clearer portion may rise and mingle with the heavenly lessons; and this comes to pass, when we present an honest soul and an upright life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For certainly it is possible for the intellect to be darkened, not only by unseasonable curiosity, but also by corrupt manners; wherefore Paul hath said to the Corinthians, “I have fed you with milk, and not with meat; for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able, for ye are yet carnal; for whereas there is among you envying, and strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal?” ( 1 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And also in the Epistle to the Hebrews, and in many places, one may see Paul asserting that this is the cause of evil doctrines; for that the soul possessed by passions cannot behold anything great or noble, but as if darkened by a sort of film suffers most grievous dimsightedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then cleanse ourselves, let us kindle the light of knowledge, let us not sow among thorns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What the thorns are, ye know, though we tell you not; for often ye have heard Christ call the cares of this present life, and the deceitfulness of riches, by this name. ( Matt. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as thorns are unfruitful, so are these things; as thorns tear those that handle them, so do these passions; as thorns are readily caught by the fire, and hateful by the husbandman, so too are the things of the world; as in thorns, wild beasts, and snakes, and scorpions hide themselves, so do they in the deceitfulness of riches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us kindle the fire of the Spirit, that we may consume the thorns, and drive away the beasts, and make the field clear for the husbandman; and after cleansing it, let us water it with the streams of the Spirit, let us plant the fruitful olive, that most kindly of trees, the evergreen, the light-giving, the nutritious, the wholesome.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All these qualities hath almsgiving, which is, as it were, a seal on those that possess it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This plant not even death when it comes causes to wither, but ever it stands enlightening the mind, feeding the sinews of the soul, and rendering its strength mightier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if we constantly possess it, we shall be able with confidence to behold the Bridegroom, and to enter into the bridal chamber; to which may we all attain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, for ever and ever.
